pi_items.source: Confirmed from Wooldridge econometrics package; dataset available at https://vincentarelbundock.github.io/Rdatasets/csv/wooldridge/mroz.csv
Variable confirmed in Mroz dataset; represents number of children under age 6
All variables verified to exist in Mroz dataset with correct meanings: educ=years of schooling, age=woman's age, exper=actual labor market experience, nwifeinc=non-wife income
Computed by fitting Poisson GLM with statsmodels on actual Mroz data (753 observations); mean of fitted values from model: glm(kidslt6 ~ const + educ + age + exper + nwifeinc, family=Poisson())

Poisson regression coefficient for education: +0.052353 (p-value=0.154). Actual model shows positive relationship between education and expected number of children under 6, contrary to claimed negative relationship.
Mroz dataset confirmed to have 753 observations from Wooldridge package documentation and direct data inspection

privileged_info_summary: The Mroz dataset is real, publicly available from Wooldridge's econometrics package, and contains 753 observations with the required variables: kidslt6 (number of children under age 6), educ (years of schooling), age, exper (labor market experience), and nwifeinc (non-wife income). When fitting a Poisson regression with kidslt6 as the dependent variable and the specified regressors, the mean of fitted values is 0.237716, confirming the metadata claim of 0.2377. However, the education coefficient is positive (+0.052353), contradicting the metadata's claim that it should be negative.
